transcript: Hi, how are you? Hey, good. How are you? Good. Tell us who you are and where you're from. I'm Kennedy Anderson I'm 17 and I'm from Crozet, Virginia. Tell us about growing up in Crozet, so I actually didn't grow up there. I move a lot because of my dad's job He's in the football industry. So I'm never in one place for a very long time. I have been moving basically my whole life. It's so hard starting over and readjusting to new places. It's tough sometimes, it really is, and it can get really lonely. Having a big family has helped me a lot. I'm one of six children, I'm second oldest. We're all pretty close, and we've been through a lot together. Everyone in my family is really athletic athletic and I'm the musical one. Music really is my therapy. Whenever I'm frustrated, whenever I'm feeling down, music has always been the one thing that has stuck with me no matter what. I'm happy where I am now and I'm thankful for all of the experiences in my life that have led me to this point. I feel like there's no better time in my life than right now to audition for American Idol. Well, good deal. What are you going to sing today? So today I'm going to sing Applause by Lady Gaga. Perfect. All right. I'm playing the piano. Oh, piano. Yeah, cool. All right. I stand here waiting for you to bang the gong To crush the critics saying Is it right or is it wrong? If only fame had an IV, baby, could I better?
